methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: HistoSonics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.25B

data.narrative:
Formation Bio is a Series D biotech company founded by Benjamine Liu and Linhao Zhang, valued at $1.7B post-money after raising $372M. The analysis references a peer universe of 8 private companies and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two valuation methods triangulate to a defensible range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yielding $2.5B. The peer-median method anchors to five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—Abridge ($5.3B Series E), Ultima Genomics ($4.0B Series C), Insitro ($2.4B Series C), HistoSonics ($2.2B Series D), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B Series C)—with a median valuation of $2.4B (range $1.5B–$5.3B). Public-comp anchoring using a 7.8x P/S multiple across three public tickers cannot be applied; Formation Bio's revenue is not disclosed, limiting application of this method.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $2.9B, with a median of $2.5B. The narrow band reflects consistency between stage-progression and peer-median methods. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory (unpublished) and capital efficiency relative to the peer cohort, which spans considerable valuation dispersion ($1.5B–$5.3B) despite similar staging.
